title: Crawl Graph, Frontier State Machine, Scheduling Identity, and Invariants
description: URLs move through ad hoc arrays, visited is marked after fetch, duplicates race into workers, and retry or cancellation loses causal state. Learners cumulatively produce deterministic frontier transition model through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define discovered, rejected, queued, claimed, fetching, fetched, parsed, failed, retryable, terminal, and canceled transitions.
Atomically reserve admitted URL identity when scheduling so concurrent discovery cannot duplicate effects.
Prioritize owner commitments, depth, page classes, freshness, origin fairness, and retries with stable tie rules.
